# Trip History

> Persistent history of completed and past trips per rider and per driver, including tracking numbers, activity timelines, and position trails for audit and replay.

**Category:** Data · **Version:** 1.0.0 · **Tags:** history · trips · tracking · audit · driver-history · rider-history

## What this does

Persistent history of completed and past trips per rider and per driver, including tracking numbers, activity timelines, and position trails for audit and replay.

Specifies 5 acceptance outcomes that any implementation must satisfy, regardless of language or framework.

## Fields

- **order_id** *(text, required)* — Public identifier of the completed or past order.
- **tracking_number** *(text, required)* — Alphanumeric tracking reference visible to customers.
- **customer_id** *(text, optional)* — Reference to the customer who requested the trip.
- **driver_id** *(text, optional)* — Reference to the driver who fulfilled the trip.
- **status** *(text, required)* — Final status of the trip (completed, canceled).
- **pickup_location** *(json, optional)* — Recorded pickup location.
- **dropoff_location** *(json, optional)* — Recorded drop-off location.
- **distance** *(number, optional)* — Total trip distance in meters.
- **duration** *(number, optional)* — Total trip duration in seconds.
- **started_at** *(datetime, optional)* — When the trip started (rider picked up).
- **completed_at** *(datetime, optional)* — When the trip ended.
- **tracking_statuses** *(json, optional)* — Chronological list of activity status changes with timestamps and locations.
- **position_trail** *(json, optional)* — Sequence of GPS positions recorded during the trip for route replay.
- **page** *(number, optional)* — Pagination cursor for listing history.
- **limit** *(number, optional)* — Maximum number of results per page.

## What must be true

- **rule_01:** Trip history includes all orders associated with a given customer or driver, regardless of current status.
- **rule_02:** Queries can be filtered by status (completed, canceled), date range, customer ID, or driver ID.
- **rule_03:** Each order in history carries its tracking number, status, route summary, and timestamps.
- **rule_04:** Tracking statuses (activity log) are stored per order and ordered chronologically.
- **rule_05:** Position trail records are stored per order/driver and enable route replay with up to 50-meter resolution.
- **rule_06:** History is paginated; a limit and cursor/page parameter control the result window.
- **rule_07:** Operators can access all history within their organization; customers and drivers see only their own.

## Success & failure scenarios

**✅ Success paths**

- **Driver History Retrieved** — when authenticated request to list orders filtered by driver_id, then Driver sees their trip history with status, distance, duration, and timestamps.
- **Customer History Retrieved** — when authenticated request to list orders filtered by customer_id, then Customer sees all their past rides with tracking numbers and final statuses.
- **Tracking Timeline Retrieved** — when request for tracking statuses for a specific order, then Full activity timeline for the trip is returned.
- **Position Trail Retrieved** — when request for position history for a specific order and driver, then Route of the trip can be replayed on a map.

**❌ Failure paths**

- **Order Not Found** — when requested order ID does not exist or belongs to a different organization, then 404 response is returned. *(error: `ORDER_NOT_FOUND`)*

## Errors it can return

- `ORDER_NOT_FOUND` — The specified order could not be found.

## Connects to

- **ride-request-lifecycle** *(required)* — Every entry in trip history is a completed order from the lifecycle.
- **driver-location-streaming** *(recommended)* — Position records that make up the position trail are written during location streaming.
- **order-trip-state-machine** *(recommended)* — Tracking statuses are written at each state machine transition.
- **customer-app-flow** *(recommended)* — Customer app exposes trip history to riders.
- **driver-app-flow** *(recommended)* — Driver app exposes trip history to drivers.
